nodes.label: Track B — adaptive readout: find the extractor's end cheaply
nodes.rationale: The reframe's core: the useful representation lives at a task-dependent depth; put readouts at multiple depths and learn which to trust — three mature literatures (deep supervision, early-exit, learned halting) map almost exactly onto the author's decider-GD idea.

nodes.label: Deep supervision heads (DSN / Mono-Forward-style)
nodes.rationale: Attach a tiny classifier/companion loss to every hidden layer; Mono-Forward IS the forward-only/local version of this already proven substrate-faithful — adopted as the base layer of the whole readout track.

nodes.label: Early-exit confidence gate (BranchyNet -> SDN -> CALM)
nodes.rationale: Exit at the first head confident enough (entropy threshold); CALM adds a calibrated statistical guarantee on quality drop from exiting early — the deployable, cheapest form of 'where to read', restoring the per-sample 80/20.

nodes.label: Learned halting (ACT / PonderNet) — CUT from Stage 1
nodes.rationale: PonderNet's halting head is the author's decider-GD done with an unbiased gradient, and the halting confidence is literally the north-star 'I-get-it feeling'; POST-REVIEW: cut from Stage-1 path (over-engineering) in favor of a calibrated confidence threshold (CALM-style); held as the north-star seed.

nodes.label: Conditional compute (MoE / SkipNet / SSM)
nodes.rationale: MoE routes width, SkipNet/BlockDrop skip depth per input, SSM/Mamba is input-dependent computation — secondary to B1-B3 for the depth problem, the broader 'lazy brain' track parked in the replan's later tiers.

nodes.label: Extractor-length profiler (per-layer linear probe)
nodes.rationale: Per-layer probe/per-head accuracy vs depth per task; defines readout placement as 'the probe's peak / where the slope flattens' — operationalizes the author's intuition, the ground truth every Track-B gate approximates online.
